title: MAP 6.13: Specify liveness reaper and mission-control TUI
description: Wave 2.5 from MAP 6.13 plan. Specify heartbeat/liveness state, reaper behavior for silent agents, and a mission-control TUI that gives the operator vitals, roster heartbeat dots, task flight board, attention queue, event stream, drill-down, and intervention affordances. Read MAP_System/artifacts/planning/map-613-master-implementation-plan.md, MAP_System/HUMAN_INTERFACE_SYSTEM.md, MAP_System/agents/status.json, MAP_System/scripts/limit_watcher.py, and Guidelines/6.13/Claude Notes mission-control section before editing.
acceptance_criteria: Liveness spec defines last_seen, active task, lane, state vocabulary, stale timeout, reaper action, dead-letter behavior, and circuit-breaker signal.
Mission-control TUI spec defines vitals bar, roster panel, flight board, attention queue, event stream, trace drill-down, and intervention keybindings.
Spec states the TUI reads durable MAP/hcom state and must not become a second source of truth.
README or planning note names Python Textual/k9s-style prototype path and explicitly defers write interventions until read-only state is correct.
